name: igraph (Python Graph Analysis Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-2.0); C library with Python bindings

performance.notes: C backend; 5-10x faster than NetworkX for large graphs; millions of nodes feasible

agent_readiness.notes: High-performance graph analysis with C backend. import igraph as ig. g = ig.Graph.Erdos_Renyi(n=100, p=0.1). g.degree(). g.betweenness(). g.community_multilevel(). g.shortest_paths(). Layouts: g.layout('fr'). ig.plot(g). Algorithms: PageRank, clustering, centrality, community detection (Louvain, leiden via leidenalg). Read/write: GraphML, GML, edgelist, Pajek. Arrow interface for data exchange. Faster than NetworkX but less Pythonic API.

gotchas: API differs from NetworkX — not a drop-in replacement
GPL-2.0 license may restrict commercial use
Vertex/edge indexing is 0-based integers (not names by default)
Plot output requires cairo (pip install cairocffi)
best_when: Large-scale graph analysis where NetworkX is too slow
Centrality algorithms on graphs with millions of edges
When C-backed performance matters for graph operations
avoid_when: When NetworkX API compatibility is needed
GPL licensing is problematic for commercial products
Simple graph operations where NetworkX ergonomics suffice
summary: igraph is a C-backed graph library with Python bindings — 5-10x faster than NetworkX for large graphs. g = ig.Graph.Erdos_Renyi(100, 0.1). g.community_multilevel() for Louvain community detection. Algorithms: PageRank, betweenness, clustering, shortest paths, centrality. Reads/writes GraphML, GML, edgelist. Use leidenalg for Leiden algorithm. GPL-2.0. Faster than NetworkX but with a different API style.
